name: WordPress Developer MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for WordPress development workflows. Enables AI agents to manage WordPress sites via WP-CLI — creating posts/pages, managing plugins and themes, running WP-CLI commands, querying the WordPress database, and automating common WordPress development tasks.
use_cases: Managing WordPress content (posts, pages, categories) from content agent workflows
Installing, updating, and managing plugins and themes via WP-CLI commands
Automating WordPress site administration tasks from DevOps agents
Querying and updating WordPress database for development and maintenance
not_for: WordPress.com hosted sites without SSH/WP-CLI access
Production WordPress changes without proper review workflow
best_when: A WordPress developer or agency managing WordPress sites wants AI agents to automate WP-CLI tasks, content management, and routine maintenance operations.
avoid_when: You use WordPress.com without server access or other CMS platforms. WP-CLI requires SSH/server access.

auth.notes: WordPress admin credentials or Application Passwords for REST API. WP-CLI requires SSH/server access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — WordPress is open source, WP-CLI is free
pricing.notes: WordPress and WP-CLI are free open source. MCP server is free open source. Hosting costs apply separately.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on WordPress server performance; no API rate limits for WP-CLI

known_agent_gotchas: WP-CLI requires server-level access — not available on managed WordPress.com hosting
WordPress admin operations affect live sites — test in staging before production use
Plugin conflicts can break WordPress — review plugin changes before applying
WordPress Application Passwords (5.6+) recommended over admin passwords for API access
agent_readiness.security_notes: WordPress admin access is powerful — use Application Passwords with minimal permissions. Avoid storing admin passwords in plaintext. Server-level access required for WP-CLI.

security_score_components.security_notes: Use Application Passwords not admin creds. Server access via SSH. Live site operations — use staging first.
